---
title: "Session timetable"
order: 1
comments: false
---

This is the **Forecasting & evaluation** course, which runs in the **second half of the SISMID week (Wednesday midday to Friday)**.
The companion [Nowcasting & $R_t$ estimation course](https://nfidd.github.io/sismid-nowcasting/) covers the first half of the week.

<!-- TODO: confirm timings (Sam/Nick) -->
The session structure below is a draft. Exact day/time allocations still need to be confirmed by the instructors.

# Day 1: Forecasting concepts and models

**Wednesday afternoon**

<!-- TODO: confirm timings (Sam/Nick) -->

## Session 0: Introduction and course overview

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-the-course-and-the-instructors): the course and the instructors
-   [Getting started](../getting-started): getting started with the course
-   [Introduction to the course](../sessions/introduction-and-course-overview): How is the course taught?
-   [Motivating the course](../sessions/slides/from-line-list-to-decisions): from an epidemiological line list to informing decisions in real-time

## Session 1: Forecasting concepts

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-forecasting): forecasting as an epidemiological problem
-   [Practice](../sessions/forecasting-concepts): basic forecasting with ARIMA models
-   Wrap up

## Session 2: Improving forecasting models

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/improving-forecasting-models): ARIMA concepts and transformations
-   [Practice](../sessions/forecasting-models): ACF plots, transformations, and seasonality
-   Wrap up

# Day 2: Forecast evaluation and ensembles

**Thursday**

<!-- TODO: confirm timings (Sam/Nick) -->

## Session 3: Forecast evaluation

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/forecast-evaluation): scoring rules and metrics
-   [Practice](../sessions/forecast-evaluation): evaluating forecasts with various metrics
-   Wrap up

## Session 4: Forecasting with ensembles

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-ensembles): strategies for collating and combining models
-   [Practice](../sessions/forecast-ensembles): evaluating methods for ensemble forecasts
-   Wrap up

# Day 3: Collaborative forecasting and course summary

**Friday**

<!-- TODO: confirm timings (Sam/Nick) -->

## Session 5: Evaluating real-world outbreak forecasts

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-real-world-forecasts): hub concepts and data structures
-   [Practice](../sessions/real-world-forecasts):
    -   evaluation example with an existing hub
    -   ensembles with an existing hub
-   Wrap up

## Session 6: Local hub playground

-   [Introduction](../sessions/slides/hub-playground): Why a local hub?
-   [Practice](../sessions/hub-playground): getting stuck in with a local hub
-   Wrap up

## Session 7: Course summary and wrap-up

-   [Summary](../sessions/slides/closing) of the course
-   Final discussion and closing
    -   [Further reading and discussion](../sessions/end-of-course-summary-and-discussion)

<!-- Note: the deck
[Introduction to the spectrum of forecasting models](../sessions/slides/introduction-to-the-spectrum-of-forecasting-models)
exists in the repository but is not currently scheduled into a session. -->
